description: Phase 1: Instant Reversal + Profit Locking. Implements tick velocity spike detection, momentum exhaustion detection, aggressive trailing on profit, and quick profit scalp mode. Also writes brain_settings.csv for EA dynamic trail overrides.
steps: 1. In config.py: Add Phase 1 parameters: TICK_VELOCITY_WINDOW=2.0 (seconds), TICK_VELOCITY_THRESHOLD=0.30 ($), EXHAUSTION_BAR_COUNT=3, AGGRESSIVE_TRAIL_PROFIT=0.50, AGGRESSIVE_TRAIL_DISTANCE=0.15, SCALP_PROFIT_THRESHOLD=0.30, SCALP_TIME_LIMIT=10.0 (seconds). Also add BRAIN_SETTINGS_FILE='neurox_v9_brain_settings.csv'.

2. In tick_collector.py: Add a _tick_timestamps deque (maxlen=50) that records time.time() for each tick alongside _tick_prices. Add method detect_velocity_spike() that checks if the price moved >= TICK_VELOCITY_THRESHOLD in <= TICK_VELOCITY_WINDOW seconds by scanning recent ticks. Returns ('BUY'|'SELL'|None, magnitude). Add method detect_exhaustion() that checks if the last EXHAUSTION_BAR_COUNT completed bars have shrinking range (bar[i].High-bar[i].Low < bar[i-1].High-bar[i-1].Low). Returns True/False.

3. In bridge.py: Add method write_brain_settings(be_profit, trail_start, trail_distance) that writes neurox_v9_brain_settings.csv with columns: setting,value. Rows: g_brain_be_profit,<val>; g_brain_trail_start,<val>; g_brain_trail_distance,<val>. This lets the EA dynamically tighten trailing.

4. In main.py: Import the new tick_collector methods. In the main loop (both tick-only and bar-based paths): (a) Call detect_velocity_spike() - if it returns a direction, immediately fire a reversal signal (skip persistence filter but keep direction lock and cooldown). (b) Call detect_exhaustion() - if True, write tightened brain_settings via bridge (be_profit=0.30, trail_start=0.50, trail_distance=0.15). If False, write normal settings. (c) Track position open times per-signal for scalp mode: if any position has profit >= $0.30 within 10 seconds of open, fire CLOSE_FULL exit signal via bridge.write_exit_signal(). For scalp profit check, compare current_price to last_signal_price based on direction. (d) Write brain_settings every loop iteration reflecting current trailing aggressiveness.
5. In config.py: Bump VERSION to '9.30' and BUILD to '20250702'.

6. Add tests/test_phase1.py: Test detect_velocity_spike() with tick data showing $0.30+ move in 2s. Test detect_exhaustion() with shrinking bars. Test write_brain_settings() creates correct CSV. Test velocity spike fires signal. Test scalp mode logic (profit >= $0.30 in <10s triggers close).
acceptance_criteria: detect_velocity_spike() returns BUY/SELL when $0.30+ move happens in 2 seconds
detect_exhaustion() returns True when 3 consecutive bars have shrinking ranges
write_brain_settings() creates proper CSV with trailing parameters
Velocity spike signals bypass persistence filter but respect cooldown and direction lock
Quick scalp mode fires CLOSE_FULL when profit >= $0.30 within 10 seconds
Config VERSION is '9.30' and BUILD is '20250702'
All new tests pass: python -m pytest tests/test_phase1.py -v
findings: All 43 new tests pass. 5 pre-existing failures in test_tick_collector (encoding issue, utf-16 vs default write) confirmed unrelated to our changes. Floating point comparison for threshold checks requires using values slightly above exact threshold (e.g., 2000.31 instead of 2000.30 for $0.30 threshold from base 2000.00). Version bumped to 9.30, build 20250702. Scalp state variables (scalp_entry_price, scalp_entry_time, scalp_direction) added as globals in main.py alongside existing position state.
